content:
# Triaxial Test

**Summary**: A destructive laboratory test to determine the mechanical properties of rock samples.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2020-gbiyanju|Gbiyanju, 2020]] performed triaxial tests to establish a baseline for rock mechanical properties.

- [[2023-al-garni|Al-Garni, 2023]] applied triaxial-test to determine Young's modulus and permeability changes in acidized carbonate rocks.

- [[2002-elhassan|ElHassan, 2002]] performed triaxial tests to obtain core sample mechanical properties.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2023-al-garni|Al-Garni, 2023]] applied triaxial-test to determine Young's modulus and permeability changes in acidized carbonate rocks.

- [[2002-elhassan|ElHassan, 2002]] performed triaxial tests to obtain core sample mechanical properties.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- Used as the standard for comparing non-destructive methods like impulse hammer testing. ([[2020-gbiyanju|Gbiyanju, 2020, p. 99]])

- Young's modulus values for AC-1 dropped at 40 MPa, indicating wormhole closure. ([[2023-al-garni|Al-Garni, 2023, p. 54]])

- Not stated in source ([[2002-elhassan|ElHassan, 2002]])
